rationale: A Space Marine Legends / Legends of the Space Marines wave: one genuine Space Marine Legends short novel (Azrael), five short stories the roster mistags as novels (Orphans of the Kraken, Twelve Wolves, The Relic, The Trial of the Mantis Warriors, The Returned — all Legends of the Space Marines 2010 stories re-released as standalone ebooks in 2016), three roster-correct short stories, and a full T'au novel mislabelled novella (Farsight: Crisis of Faith). Five format data_conflict flags result. Many are obscure shorts with thin Goodreads counts (32–166) but all carried aggregated ratings.

overrides.synopsis: The Dark Angels guard their secrets more jealously than any other Space Marine Chapter, and only their Supreme Grand Master is trusted to know them all. Azrael has fought at the forefront of battle for centuries, a living legend among his brethren. When the death of Supreme Grand Master Naberius leaves the Chapter leaderless above the world of Rhamiel, Azrael, Grand Master of the Deathwing, must shoulder the burden of command. Forced into an uneasy alliance with the enigmatic Eldar, he walks a razor's edge between the victory the Imperium demands and the buried history his Chapter would die to keep hidden. This is the story of how one warrior rose to lead the Dark Angels, and of the price that elevation exacts.

Long after the Tyranids devoured their home of Sotha and all but annihilated the Chapter, the surviving Scythes of the Emperor scrape an existence from the wreckage of past glories. Sergeant Tiresias leads a ragged salvage team of untested neophytes deep into the carcass of a dead hive ship, hunting for lost battle-brothers or whatever gear can be reclaimed. What they find instead is Commander Cassios, sole survivor of an entire company, locked in stasis since the disaster and convinced that nothing has changed, that the war against the great devourer can simply resume. As Cassios's certainty inflames the young warriors, the bitter Tiresias must weigh the future of a dying Chapter against the hunger for vengeance.

flags.reason: Roster lists format=novel, but sources show this is a short story from the Legends of the Space Marines anthology (2010), re-released as a standalone ebook in 2016.

overrides.synopsis: In the mead-halls of the Fang, a serf recounts a grim tale of the days when the apostate Cardinal Bucharis besieged the Space Wolves' fortress and overran their world. Two warriors stalk the frozen slopes of Fenris: Daegalan, a grizzled veteran, and Hrothgar, a young and eager Blood Claw. Striking from the shadows against the enemy's supply lines, the older Wolf teaches the younger the legend of the Twelve Wolves who fight beside every son of Russ. But when the pair are finally run to ground, Hrothgar reveals there is a Thirteenth Wolf few dare name, and the lesson he has to teach turns darker than any saga. A tale of brotherhood, the wild, and the curse that shadows the Space Wolves.

flags.reason: Roster lists format=novel, but sources show this is a short story from the Legends of the Space Marines anthology (2010), re-released as a standalone ebook in 2016.

overrides.synopsis: As Ghazghkull Thraka's second war for Armageddon engulfs the planet, the Black Templars hunt their foe across the frozen Dead Lands. Leading the charge is the Dreadnought Brother Jarold, an ancient warrior interred in adamantium and driven on by the loss of his Chapter's champion to a marauding Ork warband. Following a faint distress beacon buried in the ice, the Templars unearth another Dreadnought: Venerable Brother Rhodomanus of the Crimson Fists, entombed and dormant for half a century since the previous war. When the greenskins descend in overwhelming numbers, the two relics of a bygone age stand together for one final, defiant battle against the bestial tide.

flags.reason: Roster lists format=novel, but sources show this is a short story from the Legends of the Space Marines anthology (2010), re-released as a standalone ebook in 2016.

overrides.synopsis: In the bitter aftermath of the Badab War, the Mantis Warriors stand accused of treason against the Imperium. Their Chapter Master, Neotera, faces a panel of unseen judges, fellow Chapter Masters, Librarians and agents of the Inquisition, who will decide whether his brethren are traitors or merely the misled. As he relives the long road that led the Chapter astray, Neotera recalls how the Mantis Warriors were sent to investigate the renegade Astral Claws, how their lord Lugft Huron seduced them with talk of a corrupt Imperium, and the fateful moment that bound them to rebellion. Caught between defiance and remorse, Neotera wrestles with a question he cannot answer: were they ever truly guilty at all?

flags.reason: Roster lists format=novel, but sources show this is a short story from the Legends of the Space Marines anthology (2010), re-released as a standalone ebook in 2016.

flags.suggestion: The Trial of the Mantis Warriors
flags.reason: Roster title is singular ('Mantis Warrior'); the published title is plural ('Mantis Warriors').

overrides.synopsis: On the world of Gathis, the Doom Eagles honour their dead and then forget them, for their creed begins with the words 'we are already dead.' So what is to be done when one of the fallen returns? Veteran Sergeant Tarikus was thought lost when the ship carrying him was destroyed, but he was taken alive, sold to the Red Corsairs and delivered into the hands of the monstrous Fabius Bile, who tortured and remade him before he finally escaped. Now home at last, Tarikus finds no welcome, only suspicion. Set to watch him for any trace of corruption, his battle-brothers must decide whether the warrior who came back is still one of their own, or something that should never have returned.

flags.reason: Roster lists format=novel, but sources show this is a short story from the Legends of the Space Marines anthology (2010), re-released as a standalone ebook in 2016.

overrides.synopsis: When their Thunderhawk goes down over the jungles of Armageddon, only a handful of Flesh Tearers survive, and one of them is already lost. Brother Jarl has succumbed to the Black Rage, the curse that haunts the sons of Sanguinius, and has vanished into the wilds in a fury of madness and slaughter. Brother Zavien sets out alone to track him, following a trail of butchered bodies to the ruined village of Dryfield, where he must do the unthinkable and end his brother's rampage himself. But the killing does not absolve him: the Sisters of Battle who arrive in his wake see only a monster among the dead, and mete out their own merciless justice. A stark meditation on how the Imperium fears the very warriors who bleed for it.

On a world torn apart by Chaos, a father and his young son shelter in a bunker beneath their home, the last living souls in a city of ash. When they finally venture out into the ruin of their planet, they stumble upon a gravely wounded Space Marine, a warrior who needs their help to reach his Chapter and call down salvation before the world is lost to damnation forever. Told largely through the eyes of ordinary people rather than the superhuman Astartes, this is a quiet, harrowing story of faith and sacrifice at the very end of all things, and of the price two civilians pay to give their dying world one last chance.

flags.reason: Roster lists the author as 'Phil Kearney'; the story is by Paul Kearney.
Sources do not clearly name the wounded Space Marine's Chapter; tagged only at the Adeptus Astartes browse-root.

overrides.synopsis: A strike force of Salamanders led by Sergeants Dak'ir and Tsu'gan, with the Librarian Pyriel at their side, answers a call to the embattled world of Vaporis, where the Imperial Guard's 135th Phalanx are losing a grinding war against Chaos-tainted rebels in the city of Aphium. But a far older horror is stirring. As night falls, the boundary between the living and the dead grows thin: ghostly bells toll across the battlefield and spectres rise from the earth to drag soldiers screaming beneath the ground. Bolter and blade are useless against them, and only Pyriel's psychic might holds the dead at bay. Trapped below the city, the wounded Dak'ir uncovers the buried tragedy that gave Hell Night its name.

When the young and gifted Commander Farsight is chosen to lead a crusade across the Damocles Gulf, the T'au Empire expects an easy reconquest of its lost colonies. With a mighty fleet and weapons beyond anything their foes can muster, failure seems impossible. Yet behind a parade of early victories, Farsight finds enemies he never anticipated: the brutal, uncompromising armies of mankind, the unsettling secrets carried within his own expedition, and a darkness that preys on the certainties of the Greater Good. As the campaign grinds on, his courage, his loyalty, and his very soul are tested to breaking. A rare look at the grim galaxy through xenos eyes, and the making of one of the T'au's greatest and most dangerous heroes.

flags.reason: Roster lists format=novella; Lexicanum and the ~816-rating Goodreads work present Farsight: Crisis of Faith as a full-length novel.
